title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Easy Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil with our easy recipe. Learn to bake this classic Italian bread, perfect for any meal. Get the best tips!
longTailKeywords: how to make Focaccia Genovese Rosemary
Focaccia Genovese Rosemary step by step
best Focaccia Genovese Rosemary at home
lowCompetitionKeywords: Ligurian focaccia with rosemary and olive oil
seo.h1: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary and Olive Oil
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil, golden brown and perfectly dimpled.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a slice of Italy with this authentic Focaccia Genovese, fragrant with rosemary and rich olive oil – perfect for sharing!

description: Experience the true taste of Liguria with this authentic Focaccia Genovese, a beloved Italian flatbread. Infused with aromatic rosemary and rich olive oil, its signature dimpled surface promises a delightful texture and irresistible flavor. Perfect as an appetizer, side, or light meal, this recipe brings the essence of Italian baking directly to your kitchen.

ingredients.notes: plus extra for drizzling and pan

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water and yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active. This step is crucial for a good rise.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Gradually add 2 tablespoons of olive oil and continue mixing until the dough comes together. Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface.

instructions.title: Knead and First Rise
instructions.text: Knead the dough for about 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape the Focaccia
instructions.text: Gently punch down the risen dough and transfer it to a 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan generously coated with olive oil. Using your fingertips, gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it completely. Cover and let it rest for another 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Dimple and Top
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 220C (425F). Uncover the focaccia. Using your oiled fingertips, create deep indentations all over the surface of the dough. Drizzle generously with more olive oil, sprinkle with fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Bake the focaccia in the preheated oven for 20-30 minutes, or until it's golden brown on top and cooked through. The bottom should also be nicely browned. If it browns too quickly, you can cover it loosely with foil.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the focaccia from the oven and carefully transfer it to a wire rack to cool slightly. Drizzle with a final touch of extra virgin olive oil if desired. Serve warm or at room temperature, cut into squares or strips.

tips: For the most authentic Genovese focaccia, use a high-quality extra virgin olive oil. Its flavor is central to the recipe.
Do not skip the dimpling step; it creates pockets for the olive oil and gives focaccia its characteristic texture.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh on the day it's baked. To store, wrap tightly in plastic wrap at room temperature for up to 2 days. Reheat briefly in the oven for best results.
article: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a beloved Italian Baking dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
